target.name: Rain Industries
target.founder: Bryan Hatton, Ephraim Nowak, Maxwell Brodie

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Rain Industries, a Seed-stage drone and autonomous systems company, has raised $14.7M to date across its founding team of Bryan Hatton, Ephraim Nowak, and Maxwell Brodie. The broader Drones & Autonomous sector comprises seven private peers including Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Elroy Air, with no publicly traded comparables available for direct benchmarking.

Valuation analysis relies on a stage-progression methodology, as peer-median comparison is not applicable. The fragmented Drones & Autonomous landscape yielded insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables to support a reliable median approach; the five sector peers identified lack consistent data clustering by stage or valuation tier. The stage-progression method applies a 2.5x multiplier to Rain's $15M raised proxy, implying a post-money valuation of $37M. This multiplier reflects typical Seed-to-Series A step-up dynamics within frontier technology.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $31M to $42M, with a median of $37M. Key uncertainties include the absence of tight peer comparables to validate stage-appropriate multiples and limited visibility into Rain's revenue traction or unit economics relative to the broader sector. A narrower range would require either additional market data on Drones & Autonomous Seed-stage outcomes or updated company metrics demonstrating differentiated progress metrics relative to peers.
